description: Plain semantic index for MdWrk docs and product update pages.

entries.title: MdWrk
entries.description: MdWrk is a privacy-first, offline-capable Markdown workspace for writing, previewing, and managing Markdown on your device.

entries.title: MdWrk Vs VS Code
entries.description: Compare MdWrk and VS Code when you need Markdown editing in either a purpose-built writing workspace or a general developer IDE.

entries.title: MdWrk Vs Logseq
entries.description: Compare MdWrk and Logseq when choosing between Markdown workspace authoring and an outline-first knowledge graph workflow.

entries.title: MdWrk Vs MarkText
entries.description: Compare MdWrk and MarkText when choosing between a local-first Markdown workspace with reusable packages and a focused desktop Markdown editor.

entries.title: MdWrk Vs Notion
entries.description: Compare MdWrk and Notion when choosing between local Markdown files and a hosted collaborative workspace.

entries.title: MdWrk Vs Obsidian
entries.description: Compare MdWrk and Obsidian when you need local Markdown writing, preview, workspace management, extensions, and package-friendly developer surfaces.

entries.title: MdWrk Vs Standard Markdown Editors
entries.description: Compare MdWrk with standard Markdown editors when you need more than a single-file writing surface: workspace management, preview policy, extensions, themes, and reusable packages.

entries.title: MdWrk Vs Typora
entries.description: Compare MdWrk and Typora across Markdown authoring surfaces, workspace-oriented preview/editor flows, and minimal single-document writing apps.

entries.title: MdWrk Vs Zettlr
entries.description: Compare MdWrk and Zettlr when choosing between a local-first Markdown workspace and a research-oriented Markdown writing environment.

entries.title: UI Refresh 1.3.28
entries.description: MdWrk 1.3.28 improves editing ergonomics, toolbar grouping, markdown list rendering, status footer behavior, and workspace action placement.

entries.title: UI Refresh 1.3.29
entries.description: This release focuses on visual clarity, modal usability, and responsive behavior improvements-

entries.title: Extension Authoring
entries.description: Extension authoring covers manifest shape, host APIs, activation lifecycle, package-local source, generated bundles, and trust metadata for MdWrk extensions.

entries.title: Language Pack Authoring
entries.description: Language pack authoring uses the first-party studio package, locale catalogs, validation flows, export behavior, and host surfaces for MdWrk localization.

entries.title: Authoring Overview
entries.description: MdWrk authoring documentation groups extension, theme pack, and language pack workflows into focused surfaces for package and product contributors.

entries.title: Theme Pack Authoring
entries.description: Theme pack authoring uses MdWrk theme contracts, Theme Studio workflows, token exports, and package-ready surfaces for consistent editor and preview styling.

entries.title: Extension Platform
entries.description: MdWrk extensions are governed through manifest metadata, host APIs, runtime activation, manager UX, settings, permissions, and compatibility checks.

entries.title: Theme Studio and Host Surfaces
entries.description: Theme Studio demonstrates how first-party extensions use host surfaces, theme contracts, workspace panes, and export flows inside the MdWrk extension model.

entries.title: Browser Use
entries.description: The browser path runs the MdWrk client directly in a supported browser, keeps documents local by default, and remains usable offline after app assets are cached.

entries.title: Client Configuration
entries.description: Theme Selection Open Settings -> Theme to switch between built-in theme families. Themes are contract-backed and flow through the editor, preview, export, and extension theme APIs.

entries.title: Client Installation
entries.description: Use the browser route for immediate access, the PWA route for an installable shell, local setup for development control, or standalone modules for package-level adoption.

entries.title: Local Setup
entries.description: This path is for maintainers, internal adopters, and self-hosting operators who want more control than the browser or PWA flows provide.

entries.title: PWA Installation
entries.description: The Progressive Web App path keeps the MdWrk client in the web platform, but lets supported browsers install it as a dedicated app window.

entries.title: Standalone Modules
entries.description: MdWrk standalone modules expose the editor, renderer, extension contracts, theme tokens, and installable extension packages as reusable package surfaces.

entries.title: GitHub Sync
entries.description: Repository Access Use a GitHub personal access token with the narrowest permissions your workflow requires. Store the token only when you want mdwrk to read from or write to a repository.

entries.title: Markdown Formatting
entries.description: MdWrk uses a CommonMark plus GFM baseline, exposes optional formatting profiles for footnotes, math, and related syntax, and adds opinionated editor tools for faster authoring without locking documents into a proprietary format.

entries.title: Developer Documentation
entries.description: MdWrk developer documentation covers local setup, standalone packages, editor and renderer modules, extension authoring, theme packs, language packs, and package-level adoption paths.

entries.title: Editor Basics
entries.description: MdWrk combines a markdown source editor, split preview workflow, table builder, formatting toolbar, and local-first workspace model so drafting stays fast without hiding the underlying Markdown.

entries.title: Extension Host
entries.description: MdWrk includes a governed extension host with manifest, runtime, capability, trust, settings, command, view, diagnostics, theme, and language-pack surfaces.

entries.title: Local-First Markdown Workspace
entries.description: MdWrk is a local-first Markdown workspace for projects, folders, files, preview links, themes, and extensions that should remain usable without forcing a cloud document model.

entries.title: Markdown File Manager
entries.description: MdWrk manages Markdown projects, files, folders, preview navigation, and workspace entries so documents stay organized inside the same surface where they are written.

entries.title: Markdown Preview Editor
entries.description: MdWrk combines a Markdown source editor with live preview, profile-aware rendering, internal-link navigation, HTML export, and print-oriented output.

entries.title: Offline Markdown Editor
entries.description: MdWrk is an offline-capable Markdown editor that keeps writing, preview, workspace state, and installable app behavior available on the device after the client assets are loaded.

entries.title: Privacy-First Markdown Editor
entries.description: MdWrk is a privacy-first Markdown editor that keeps documents on the device by default and makes sync, export, extension trust, and external services explicit choices.

entries.title: Rendering and Preview
entries.description: MdWrk turns Markdown into a live rendered preview with internal-link navigation, profile-aware HTML handling, policy advisories, and export paths that stay aligned with the on-screen renderer.

entries.title: Theme Packs
entries.description: MdWrk theme packs use a shared token and class-name contract so editor, renderer, extension, and workspace surfaces can be styled without private app selectors.

entries.title: Build with the Same MdWrk Editor and Renderer the Client Ships
entries.author: CobyCloud
entries.description: MdWrk packages its editor and renderer as reusable modules, giving product teams a direct path to embed the same authoring and preview surfaces the client uses every day.
headings: Repository history that shapes the package surface
Who should use this package family

image.alt: MdWrk editor and preview running side by side

entries.description: MdWrk expands quickly with responsive layout tuning, keyboard navigation, export actions, print flows, and richer markdown rendering, giving teams more range across screens and daily writing tasks.

entries.title: Retained Client Versions and the Desktop Shell Expand How Teams Can Deploy MdWrk

entries.title: Screenshot Matrix Coverage Helps MdWrk Prove Themes Across Viewports and Workflows

entries.title: Settings Simplification Gives MdWrk a Faster Path from Installation to Daily Use

entries.description: MdWrk refines its settings UX with cleaner labels and a calmer navigation model, helping users move from install to configuration with more momentum and less friction.

entries.title: Workspace Files and Git Operations Packages Open a Larger Automation Surface for MdWrk
entries.author: CobyCloud
entries.description: MdWrk expands its package platform with workspace-files and Git-operations modules, giving teams more room to automate file-aware and repository-aware workflows inside the client.
